title: Starfire Direct Product Media and SEO Support
summary: Product media, Shopify execution, creative roadmap support, and SEO/SEM collaboration for a high-volume eCommerce environment.

metrics.label: Month-over-month organic traffic growth supported through SEO/SEM work
Year-to-date visibility improvement noted on the resume

challenge: Create high-quality product media while also supporting merchandising, SEO hygiene, and broader go-to-market execution inside a performance-driven eCommerce team.
approach: Developed product imagery, line drawings, and ad creative suitable for both merchandising and campaign use.
Handled Shopify uploads with SEO-aware file naming and alt text conventions.
Partnered with marketing leadership on redesign work and media roadmaps aligned to performance priorities.
Audited listings and visual standards so assets stayed consistent with the brand’s eCommerce expectations.
results: Supported the build-out of an SEO/SEM program tied to measurable traffic and visibility gains.
Strengthened the product-detail and merchandising layer with cleaner media operations and better asset consistency.
Contributed to both daily creative production and larger site-level improvement work.

sections.heading: Where design met operations
paragraphs: This role is one of the clearest examples of Erroll’s value in eCommerce: not just designing assets, but making sure those assets work inside the platform. Product imagery, alt text, file naming, uploads, audits, and merchandising support all mattered because execution quality affected discovery and conversion.
That combination of design and backend reliability is what makes this work relevant to Shopify brands and growth teams that need more than surface-level visuals.

sections.heading: Why the work stands out
paragraphs: The strongest signal here is range. The role touched creative production, Shopify operations, redesign collaboration, and SEO support. That is exactly the kind of hybrid operator profile many eCommerce teams want but struggle to hire.
